oneOf.description:
Single brand with no sub-brands or product lines. The brand IS the
product (KICKGEIST, most B2C apps). Distinct from `BrandedHouse`,
which has sub-brands under a master brand.

approval_process.description:
Prose description of how approvals flow from request to decision
(who reviews, in what order, with which artefacts). Optional for
gradual adoption, but strongly recommended alongside `owner`:
without it, `review_required_for` has no enforceable mechanism.

review_required_for.description:
Change types that trigger formal review before publication. Typical
entries: `logo-modifications`, `primary-palette-changes`,
`naming-new-sub-brand`, `co-branding-agreements`. Keep entries
imperative and specific so AI agents and humans agree on scope.

approved_by.description:
PathRef to the person or role who formally approved this version.
Optional because early versions and solo-founder brands may lack a
separate approver; populate as soon as governance exists so the audit
trail is complete.

max_size_ratio.description:
Maximum size ratio of the partner mark relative to this brand's mark.
1.0 = equal size (endorsement-parity). >1.0 allows the partner to be
visually larger (e.g. co-sponsored event where the partner is primary).
Capped at 5.0 because anything beyond that effectively hides this brand.

min_clear_space_px.description: Minimum clear space between the two marks in pixels. Non-negative.

NamingConvention.description: Naming convention rules. Includes examples (D2: non-derivable).

examples.description:
Concrete examples of names that satisfy `pattern`. Kept explicit
because examples cannot be derived from the pattern alone (D2).

max_characters.description:
Optional upper bound on full product/sub-brand name length in
characters. Drives UI truncation decisions and trademark screening.

pattern.description:
Human-readable naming pattern for sub-brands and products. May be a
regex, a template like `{Master} {ProductNoun}`, or a prose rule.
Consumed by reviewers and AI assistants during naming decisions.

prohibited_patterns.description:
Patterns that must NOT be used (e.g. trademarked terms, reserved
prefixes, competitor names, abbreviations with negative connotations).

PathRef.description:
Path-based cross-reference relative to .corpospec/ root.
Pattern: `^[a-z0-9_-]+(/[a-z0-9_.-]+)+$`

visual_independence_level.description:
Visual independence from the master brand on a 1-5 scale.
1 = identical visual system (branded house, e.g. Google Maps).
3 = shared core with distinct treatment (endorsed).
5 = fully independent visual identity (house of brands).
Omit when the architecture model alone adequately describes the
relationship and a numeric score would be noise.
